---
title: "Genetic diversity"
output:
  github_document:
    toc: TRUE
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(ggpubr)
library(algatr)
library(viridis)
library(tigris)
library(raster)
library(igraph)
library(sp)
library(gridExtra)
library(wingen)
devtools::load_all()

# Set global options for caching
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  cache = TRUE,
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  result = FALSE
)

source(here("analysis", "genetic_diversity", "genetic_diversity.R"))
source(here("general_functions.R"))
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Load the U.S. state boundaries data
ca <- get_ca()
dem <- get_dem()
coords <- get_coords(sf = TRUE)
```


# Heterozygosity
```{r heterozygosity, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 7}
# get het data
het <- get_het()

# Combine coords and het data
coords <- 
  left_join(coords, het, by = "SampleID") %>% 
  drop_na(Ho)

ggplot(coords) + 
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Ho), col = "#00000087", pch = 21, cex = 3) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako") +
  theme_void()
```

```{r heterozygosity window, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 7, eval = FALSE}
# Project coordinates and boundary to equal area projection (California Albers)
coords_proj <- st_transform(coords, 3310)
ca_proj <- st_transform(ca, 3310)

# Convert coordinates to a raster layer with 10km resolution
lyr <- coords_to_raster(coords_proj, buffer = 10, res = 10000)

# Calculate windowed mean of Ho
window_Ho <- window_general(coords_proj$Ho, coords = coords_proj, lyr = lyr, wdim = 9, stat = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

# Plot windowed mean of Ho
ggplot_gd(window_Ho, bkg = ca_proj, col = magma(100))

# Perform kriging interpolation on windowed mean of Ho
krig_Ho <- krig_gd(window_Ho, grd = lyr, disagg_grd = 2)

# Apply a mask to the kriging result
count_mask <- resample(window_Ho$sample_count, krig_Ho)
mask_Ho <- mask_gd(krig_Ho, ca_proj)
mask_Ho <- mask_gd(mask_Ho, count_mask, minval = 0.1)

# Plot masked kriging result
ggplot_gd(mask_Ho, bkg = ca_proj, col = mako(100)) +
  labs(fill = "Ho")


ggplot_gd(window_Ho, bkg = ca_proj, col = mako(100)) +
  labs(fill = "Ho")
```

# ROH
```{r ROH, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 7}
roh <- get_roh()

ggplot(roh) +
 geom_sf(data = ca) +
 geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry, fill = froh), col = "#00000087", pch = 21, cex = 2.5) +
 scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", labels = scales::label_number(scale = 1, accuracy = 0.001)) +
 theme_void()

ggplot(roh) +
 geom_sf(data = ca) +
 geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry, fill = log(froh + 1e-6)), col = "#00000087", pch = 21, cex = 2.5) +
 scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", labels = scales::label_number(scale = 1, accuracy = 0.001)) +
 theme_void()

ggplot(roh) +
 geom_sf(data = ca) +
 geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry, fill = froh0), col = "#00000087", pch = 21, cex = 2.5) +
 scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", labels = scales::label_number(scale = 1, accuracy = 0.001)) +
 labs(fill = "FROH") +
 theme_void()

ggplot(roh) +
 geom_sf(data = ca) +
 geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry, fill = log(froh0 + 1e-6)), col = "#00000087", pch = 21, cex = 2.5) +
 scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", labels = scales::label_number(scale = 1, accuracy = 0.001)) +
 labs(fill = "log(FROH + 1e-6)") +
 theme_void()

ggplot(roh) +
 geom_sf(data = ca) +
 geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry, col = pop), cex = 2.5) +
 theme_void()

ggplot(roh) +
 geom_sf(data = ca) +
 geom_raster(data = dem, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = elev)) + 
 geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry, col = log(froh + 1e-6)), pch = 16, cex = 2.5) +
 scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno", labels = scales::label_number(scale = 1, accuracy = 0.001)) +
 scale_fill_gradient(low = "gray40", high = "white", na.value = NA) +
 theme_void()

```


```{r roh window, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 7, eval = FALSE}
# project roh 
coords_proj <- roh %>% st_as_sf() %>% st_transform(3310)

# Calculate windowed mean of froh
window_froh <- window_general(coords_proj$froh0, coords = coords_proj, lyr = lyr, wdim = 9, stat = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

# Plot windowed mean of froh
ggplot_gd(window_froh, bkg = ca_proj, col = magma(100))

# Perform kriging interpolation on windowed mean of froh
krig_froh <- krig_gd(window_froh, grd = lyr, disagg_grd = 2)

# Apply a mask to the kriging result
count_mask <- resample(window_froh$sample_count, krig_froh)
mask_froh <- mask_gd(krig_froh, ca_proj)
#mask_froh <- mask_gd(mask_froh, count_mask, minval = 1)

# Plot masked kriging result
ggplot_gd(mask_froh, bkg = ca_proj, col = magma(100))
```

# Models
```{r models, fig.width = 5, fig.height = 5, result = FALSE, message = FALSE, eval = TRUE}
envdata <- get_env() %>% left_join(get_coords(), by = c("x","y")) %>% rename(SampleID = SampleID.y)
mod_df <- 
  left_join(roh, envdata) %>%
  left_join(het) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(froh0_log = log(froh0 + 1)) %>%
  drop_na(froh0, Ho, elevation)

mod_df <- 
  left_join(het, envdata) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  drop_na(Ho, elevation)


# FROH
mod_div <- lm(froh0_log ~ Ho + elevation, data = mod_df)
summary(mod_div)
plot(mod_div)

mod_df$fitted <- mod_div$fitted.values
ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = Ho, y = froh0_log, col = pop)) +
  theme_classic()
ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = fitted, y = froh0_log, col = pop)) +
  theme_classic()

# Climate model:
mod_clim <- lm(froh0_log ~ CA_rPCA1 + CA_rPCA2 + CA_rPCA3, data = mod_df)
summary(mod_clim)
mod_clim2 <- lm(froh0_log ~ CA_rPCA2 , data = mod_df)
summary(mod_clim2)

# Elevation model:
mod_elev <- lm(froh0_log ~ elevation, data = mod_df)
summary(mod_elev)

# Elevation and CA_rPCA2 are correlated
mat <- as.matrix(drop_na(mod_df[,c("CA_rPCA1", "CA_rPCA2", "CA_rPCA3", "elevation")]))
mod_clim_elev <- lm(froh0_log ~ CA_rPCA1 + elevation + CA_rPCA3, data = mod_df)
summary(mod_clim_elev)

# Development and imperviousness model
mod_imperv <- lm(froh0_log ~ nlcd_imperviousness + elevation, data = mod_df)
summary(mod_imperv)

mod_devel <- lm(froh0_log ~ developed + elevation, data = mod_df)
summary(mod_devel)

# Comparison of AIC suggests that elevation alone is the best model
AIC(mod_clim_elev)
AIC(mod_clim)
AIC(mod_elev)
AIC(mod_clim2)

# plot of elevation vs roh
# doesn't seem suggestive of a strong relationship
ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = elevation, y = froh0_log, col = pop)) +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = CA_rPCA2, y = froh0_log, col = pop)) +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = CA_rPCA1, y = froh0_log, col = pop)) +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = CA_rPCA3, y = froh0_log, col = pop)) +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r}
# HO
# Climate model:
mod_clim <- lm(Ho ~ CA_rPCA1 + CA_rPCA2 + CA_rPCA3, data = mod_df)
# CA_rPCA1 is significant
summary(mod_clim)

# Elevation model:
mod_elev <- lm(Ho ~ elevation, data = mod_df)
# Elevation is not significant...but it is close
summary(mod_elev)

# Development and imperviousness model
mod_imperv <- lm(Ho ~ nlcd_imperviousness + elevation, data = mod_df)
summary(mod_imperv)

mod_devel <- lm(Ho ~ developed + elevation, data = mod_df)
summary(mod_devel)

ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_point(aes(x = CA_rPCA1, y = Ho)) +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, col = CA_rPCA1)) +
  theme_void() +
  scale_color_viridis_c()
```

# Comparison of ROH vs Ho
```{r}

mod_df <- mod_df %>% drop_na(Ho, froh0)

# fit exponential decay
mod_exp <- nls(froh0 ~ a * exp(-b * Ho), data = mod_df, start = list(a = 1, b = 1))
summary(mod_exp)

# Generate predicted values
mod_df$predicted <- predict(mod_exp)

# Get residuals
mod_df$residuals <- as.numeric(residuals(mod_exp))

# Plot the line
ggplot(mod_df, aes(x = Ho, y = froh0)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line(aes(y = predicted), color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Exponential Decay Fit", x = "Ho", y = "froh0")

# Make sf
mod_df <- st_as_sf(mod_df)

rclone
```

# MODELS
```{r}
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
source(here("general_functions.R"))
source(here("analysis", "genetic_diversity", "genetic_diversity.R"))
coords <- get_coords(sf = TRUE)
# LOOK INTO: If you use bio1 dif instead of bio2 dif the model is super different (correlations)
source(here("analysis", "sdm", "paleovars.R"))
paleovars <- rast(here("data", "env", "paleoclim", "paleoclim.tif"))
bio2_dif <- paleovars$cur_bio_2 - paleovars$lh_bio_2
bio2_dif_vals <- terra::extract(bio2_dif, coords, ID = FALSE)[,1]
bio2_dif_df <- data.frame(SampleID = coords$SampleID, bio2_dif = bio2_dif_vals)

env_data <- rast(here("data", "env", "california_env_stack.tif"))
env_vals <- data.frame(terra::extract(env_data, coords, ID = FALSE))
env_vals$SampleID <- coords$SampleID

csi_past <- rast(here("data", "env", "stability", "Layers", "past", "csi_past.tif"))
csi_vals <- data.frame(csi = terra::extract(csi_past, coords, ID = FALSE)[,1])
csi_vals$SampleID <- coords$SampleID

q <- read_csv(here("analysis", "admixture", "outputs", "Q6.csv"))
q <- 
  q %>%
  # only keep the pop group corresponding to the cluster
  mutate(PopGroup = as.numeric(gsub("V", "", PopGroup))) %>%
  filter(cluster == PopGroup) %>%
  dplyr::select(SampleID, Q)


dem <- get_dem()

mod_df <- 
  get_coords() %>% 
  left_join(get_het()) %>%
  left_join(get_roh()) %>%
  left_join(env_vals) %>%
  left_join(bio2_dif_df) %>%
  left_join(dem) %>%
  left_join(q) %>%
  left_join(csi_vals) %>%
  mutate(BurnDate = BurnDate > 0) %>%
  drop_na(Ho, BurnDate, NDVI, gHM, bio2_dif, elev, CHELSA_bio1_1981.2010_V.2.1, Q, csi)

mod_sf <- st_as_sf(mod_df)
ggplot(mod_sf) +
  geom_sf(aes(col = BurnDate))

cor(mod_df[,c("NDVI", "gHM", "CHELSA_bio1_1981.2010_V.2.1", "elev", "BurnDate", "bio2_dif", "Q", "csi")], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# with Q
summary(lm(Ho ~ BurnDate + NDVI + gHM + CHELSA_bio1_1981.2010_V.2.1 + bio2_dif + elev + Q + csi, data = mod_df))

# without Q
summary(lm(Ho ~ BurnDate + NDVI + gHM + CHELSA_bio1_1981.2010_V.2.1 + bio2_dif + elev + csi, data = mod_df))

# Note how Q is predicted by BurnDateTRUE/CHELSA_bio1_1981.2010_V.2.1 which are the variables that drop out in the with Q model
summary(lm(Q ~ BurnDate + NDVI + gHM + CHELSA_bio1_1981.2010_V.2.1 + bio2_dif + elev + csi, data = mod_df))
# therefore, I don't think Q should be a predictor variable, but I think it is circular (already a measure of genetic variation)
# we may want to add Q models however to get models of predicted admixture


plot(mod_df$gHM, mod_df$Ho)
plot(mod_df$BurnDate, mod_df$Ho)
plot(mod_df$CHELSA_bio1_1981.2010_V.2.1, mod_df$Ho)

ggplot(mod_df,aes(x = BurnDate, y = Ho)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1) +
  theme_classic()
 
ggplot(st_as_sf(mod_df)) +
  geom_sf(aes(col = BurnDate)) 
  
```
